stories.title: TheKiddoSpace Peg Doll Recall — Choking Hazard for Under-3

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
⚠️ RECALL: TheKiddoSpace Peg Dolls in Cups wooden toy set.

Made for kids under 3. The small dolls can block a child's airway. Violates the small parts ban.

Sold at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com July 2023–Feb 2025, ~$30.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Do you have TheKiddoSpace Peg Dolls in Cups? Stop using them.

The peg pieces can block a toddler's airway. CPSC recall: write "RECALLED" on the pieces, photo to info@thekiddospace.com for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you've got a wooden peg doll set from TheKiddoSpace, it's time to pull it off the shelf.

The CPSC recalled TheKiddoSpace "Peg Dolls in Cups" — a set of seven rainbow-colored wooden peg figures designed for children under 3. The problem: the peg pieces are small enough to block a toddler's airway, and they violate the federal small parts ban. The toys were sold at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com from July 2023 through February 2025 for about $30.

Here's what to do if you have one: stop using it immediately and take it away from your kids. Write "RECALLED" in permanent marker on each piece, take a photo, and email it to info@thekiddospace.com. They'll issue a full refund.

This is exactly the kind of toy that looks completely harmless — a cute, colorful wooden set for little ones. But those small pieces are the problem. If you're not sure whether yours qualifies, check the packaging for "Peg Dolls in Cups" or visit CPSC.gov for the full details.

We know a lot of Colorado Springs families love toys like this for toddler play, so spread the word. Do you do regular checks on your kids' toys against the CPSC recall list?

---
facebook.image_caption:
⚠️ Parents: TheKiddoSpace Peg Dolls in Cups are recalled.

The small wooden peg figures can block a toddler's airway — they violate the federal small parts ban. Sold ~$30 at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com July 2023–Feb 2025.

Stop use. Write "RECALLED" on pieces, photo to info@thekiddospace.com for a full refund.

Have you checked your toy boxes against CPSC.gov recently?

article.headline: CPSC Recalls TheKiddoSpace Peg Dolls: Choking Hazard for Kids Under 3

stories.title: Bronc Day Festival Returns July 25 to Green Mountain Falls

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Bronc Day Festival is July 25 in Green Mountain Falls.

Free admission. 10 AM parade, 7:30 AM pancake breakfast, bounce house, rubber duck races, and 30+ vendors at Gazebo Lake Park.

88 years strong. 🤠

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
This year's Bronc Day theme: "Let Freedom Ring!" — honoring America's 250th and Colorado's 150th.

July 25 in Green Mountain Falls. Free entry, five food trucks, and family activities all day at Gazebo Lake Park.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
If you haven't been to Bronc Day, this is the year to go.

The 88th annual Bronc Day Festival takes over Green Mountain Falls on Saturday, July 25 — and it's completely free. This year's theme is "Let Freedom Ring!" in honor of America's 250th birthday and Colorado's 150th anniversary, and the lineup is packed.

Start the morning with a pancake breakfast from 7:30 to 10 AM at Church in the Wildwood UCC, then catch the parade at 10 AM along Ute Pass Avenue. After that, head to Gazebo Lake Park for the rest of the day: rubber duck races, princess appearances from Fairy Tale Events, a bounce house, Cool Science experiments, Dinosaur Resource Center activities, touch-a-truck, face painting, balloon animals, magic shows, bubble fun, an all-ages coloring station, and a pie contest.

More than 30 vendors will be selling handmade goods, and five food trucks will be on site — Grilled Cheese, City Dogs, Dani's Thai BBQ, Kona Ice, and Pikes Peak Funnel Cake. This year's Grand Marshal is Retired Fire Chief Steve Murphy.

Green Mountain Falls is about 20 minutes up Ute Pass from Colorado Springs — a perfect excuse to get out of town for the day. Free parking in the area, and it's a great outing for all ages.

Are you planning to take the family to Bronc Day this year?

---
facebook.image_caption:
📅 Mark your calendars: Bronc Day Festival is July 25 in Green Mountain Falls.

88 years of Ute Pass tradition. Free admission. Parade at 10 AM, pancake breakfast from 7:30 AM, live music, pie contest, rubber duck races, bounce house, 30+ vendors, and five food trucks at Gazebo Lake Park.

Theme this year: "Let Freedom Ring!" — honoring America's 250th and Colorado's 150th.

Will you be there? 🤠

article.headline: Bronc Day Festival Returns to Green Mountain Falls July 25 — 88 Years and Going Strong

stories.title: D49 Opening Student Success Center With Early Childhood Classrooms in August

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
D49 is opening a Student Success Center this August — with two new early childhood classrooms.

D49 returns August 4. D11: August 12. D20: August 13.

Back to school is closer than it feels.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Colorado Springs parents, August is almost here — and District 49 has something new waiting when school starts.

D49 is opening a Student Success Center for the 2026-27 school year, and D49 Early Childhood Education will launch two new classrooms there in August. The new facility expands early learning access for some of the youngest students in the district.

Here's a quick back-to-school timeline for COS families: D49 students return August 4, with new kindergarten, 6th, and 9th grade students getting an extra orientation day on August 3. D49 preschool starts August 10. D11 opens August 12, and D20 students head back August 13.

If you've got a little one who might be eligible for early childhood programs at D49, it's worth checking d49.org for enrollment details before those first-day dates arrive.

Summer is going by fast. Which district is your family in — and are you ready for back to school?

---
facebook.image_caption:
🎒 D49 is opening a new Student Success Center this August — with two new early childhood classrooms.

COS back-to-school dates: D49 Aug 4 (new K/6th/9th on Aug 3; preschool Aug 10) · D11 Aug 12 · D20 Aug 13

Which district are your kids in?

stories.title: AAP Updated Screen Time Guidelines 2026 — What Families Need to Know This Summer

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The AAP dropped its 2-hour screen time limit in 2026.

The new focus: content quality over clock-watching. Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes. No hard caps.

What does screen time look like in your house this summer?

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The American Academy of Pediatrics quietly rewrote the screen time rulebook this year — and the biggest change is what's no longer there.

Back in 2016, the AAP recommended no more than two hours of screen time per day for kids ages 6 and up. That guideline is gone. In February 2026, the AAP released a new framework that focuses on quality of content, context, and family conversation — not a hard clock limit.

Here's what the new guidelines actually say: no screens at all for children under 18 months (video calls with family are the exception); ages 2 to 5 can have up to one hour of high-quality programming per day with a parent watching alongside; for older kids, there's no set time limit, but screens shouldn't crowd out sleep, physical activity, family time, or free play.

The AAP now recommends making bedrooms and mealtimes device-free zones for the whole family. Co-viewing — watching or playing together rather than handing kids a device and stepping away — is the new benchmark.

Summer is a natural time to reset routines. As COS families head into the back half of July, it might be a good moment to think about what screen habits you want to carry into the school year.

What's your family's approach to screen time in the summer — strict limits, go with the flow, or something in between?

---
facebook.image_caption:
📱 The AAP updated its screen time guidelines in 2026 — and the 2-hour limit is gone.

The new focus: content quality, context, and family involvement — not the clock. Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes are the new baseline for all ages.

Under 18 months: no screens. Ages 2–5: up to 1 hour/day of quality content with a parent.

How are you managing screen time this summer?

article.headline: The AAP Dropped the 2-Hour Screen Time Rule in 2026 — Here's What Replaced It

stories.title: Discover COS Photo Contest Opens + Park and Recreation Month Events

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Colorado Springs is building the 2027 Discover COS calendar — and they want your photos. 📷

Submissions opened July 15. Parks, trails, and open spaces. All ages welcome.

Details: coloradosprings.gov/parkandrecmonth

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Colorado Springs, the city literally wants your photos.

The Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services Department just opened submissions for the 2027 Discover COS calendar on July 15. The 2027 edition will highlight the natural beauty of parks, trails, and open spaces across Colorado Springs — and community members of all ages are invited to submit.

This is Park and Recreation Month, and the city is keeping it free all July long under this year's theme: "The Power Of." Alongside the photo contest, there's also an all-month coloring contest — grab a special 2026 Park & Rec Month coloring sheet, share what you love most about parks and recreation, and you might end up on the city's social media channels.

If your family has been hitting the trails, park shelters, or open spaces this summer, this is a great way to show off Colorado Springs while potentially landing your shot in the city's official 2027 calendar.

Find details and submission info at coloradosprings.gov/parkandrecmonth.

Have you taken any photos around Colorado Springs this summer that you're especially proud of?

---
facebook.image_caption:
📷 Colorado Springs just opened submissions for the 2027 Discover COS calendar.

Parks, trails, and open spaces around COS — the city wants your best shots. Submissions opened July 15. All ages welcome.

Also: all-month coloring contest for Park and Recreation Month (theme: "The Power Of").

Submit at coloradosprings.gov/parkandrecmonth. Have you taken any great park photos this summer?

article.headline: Your Park Photos Could Make the 2027 Colorado Springs Calendar — Submissions Just Opened

No people. Clear vivid blue Colorado sky. Captures the natural beauty of Colorado Springs open spaces. Clean open bottom third for text overlay. No text, no logos, no watermarks.

A single family (seen from behind, small in frame) walking the trail in the distance. Clean open bottom third for text overlay. No text, no logos, no watermarks.
